feed.title: Q-Lock: Quantum-Resistant Spending via ECDSA + Hash-Based Secrets

entry.title: Q-Lock: Quantum-Resistant Spending via ECDSA + Hash-Based Secrets
entry.summary: The email highlights a significant aspect of the 2-transaction design, emphasizing its intentional use to prevent public key mempool exposure entirely. This approach is marked as the only viable solution to completely safeguard against such vulnerabilities. In contrast, SHRINCS is mentioned but noted as ineffective in addressing this particular issue. Additionally, there's a mention of Q-Lock which includes a 1tx fast mode to cater to users seeking performance at par with SHRINCS, yet maintains security by closing potential mempool attack windows. The discussion appears to stem from a technical debate on optimal strategies for enhancing transaction security and efficiency within blockchain operations, specifically in the context of Bitcoin development.
